articles.title: Royal Navy chief backs drones, autonomous weapons in ‘Hybrid Navy’
articles.snippet: Plan mixes crewed ships, robot escorts, and long-range strike to bolster a stretched fleet The leader of Britain’s Royal Navy has outlined a “Hybrid Navy” built on a mix of crewed, uncrewed, and autonomous platforms to ensure it can continue to defend the nation and operate overseas.…
articles.ai_summary: The Royal Navy chief has proposed a 'Hybrid Navy' that integrates crewed ships with drones and autonomous weapons to enhance operational capabilities. This approach aims to address the challenges of a stretched fleet and ensure effective defense and overseas operations.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes naval defense strategy with advanced technology integration.
